summary: iter266.14 Beverage Network backend verified end-to-end. 15/15 tests PASS covering /availability (base + alcoholic/non_alcoholic filters), /find (paloma outlet matches, ketel-one central match, exclude_outlet filtering), /transfer (happy path + 400 on same outlet + 422 on quantity<=0), /transfers (audit list + persistence of just-posted row), plus 4-endpoint regression on chef-outlet (dashboard, beo-timeline, beo-timeline/cumulative) and echo-schedule/labor-brain. No mocks — all hit real Mongo collections (beverage_inventory, foh_beverage_sales, beverage_transfers).

minor.issue: Accepts raw List[str] body, not {beo_ids: [...]}. Frontend integrators should be aware. Consider wrapping in a Pydantic model for clarity.

critical_code_review_comments: routes/beverage_network.py _is_alcoholic() classifies central_stock purely by `spirit_type` truthiness — any future non-spirit central SKU (e.g., NA beer, juice) will be misclassified as alcoholic unless seeded without spirit_type. Add an explicit `is_alcoholic` field on beverage_inventory or expand the heuristic before adding NA inventory.

POST /transfer silently swallows DB insert exceptions (try/except: pass) yet still returns ok:true. If Mongo write fails the caller has no signal. Recommend logging + returning a non-200 or a degraded flag.
GET /find returns available_qty='unknown_assume_stocked' as a string for outlet matches — frontend must handle a string|int union. Consider null + a separate boolean instead.

Sales lookback uses string ISO comparison on `created_at` which only works because all rows are stored as ISO strings; if any sales row is stored as a BSON Date, the $gte filter silently returns nothing. Recommend a normalized datetime field.
No auth on /transfer means any caller can fabricate inventory moves. Acceptable for the brief (no-auth) but flag for Tenant Admin Phase 2.
test_credentials: n/a — endpoints are unauthenticated per brief
seed_data_creation: Test transfers created with TEST_ prefix (sku=TEST_Paloma, requested_by=TEST_gm). Rows persist in beverage_transfers but are clearly tagged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter266_14_beverage_network.py is the regression suite for the Beverage Network module. The cumulative endpoint takes a bare JSON list (not an object) — keep that in mind if testing again. Central beverage_inventory seed is all alcoholic (10 SKUs); /availability?category=non_alcoholic legitimately returns an empty central_stock today.
